foresight_scores.hypothesis: Werkzeug deliberately keeps runtime dependencies to an absolute minimum (a single required dependency) so it can remain an embeddable low-level WSGI toolkit that frameworks like Flask build on, without forcing template-engine/database/etc. choices onto downstream applications.

The maintainers intentionally treat 'importable from the module' as the de facto public API contract instead of curating an explicit `__all__` export list, accepting that most module-level names are effectively public and must be handled through docstring/versionchanged discipline rather than access control.

Security-sensitive modules (password hashing, safe path joining) are deliberately implemented using only the Python standard library, avoiding third-party crypto/path dependencies, to minimize the supply-chain attack surface of the most safety-critical code paths.
